analysis: Better heuristics prioritize pre‐processing (e.g., dominated point pruning, skyline methods) and leverage shared Monte Carlo samples to estimate marginal contributions. They use incremental updates with vectorized operations to avoid costly full hypervolume computations, combine greedy selection with local swap refinements, and employ tie-breaking via box volume. In contrast, weaker solutions repeatedly invoke heavy hypervolume calculations or lack efficient data reuse, leading to unnecessary overhead.
experience: Focus on candidate pruning, reuse of sampled data, and vectorized incremental updates. Minimize repeated expensive hypervolume calls by using approximate evaluations and limited local refinements on shortlisted candidates.
current_reflection: Focus on candidate pruning, reuse of sampled data, and vectorized incremental updates. Minimize repeated expensive hypervolume calls by using approximate evaluations and limited local refinements on shortlisted candidates.
bad_reflections: Focus on early pruning, vectorized operations, and shared sampling to reduce overhead. Use block-chunking and caching for expensive computations, and integrate small-budget local refinements to polish results efficiently.

Focus on efficient pre‐processing (like skyline pruning) and reuse MC samples with vectorized, incremental updates. Limit expensive exact hypervolume calls by confining local swaps to small candidate shortlists, thus balancing quality and speed.

Focus on aggressive pre-pruning and reuse shared samples with vectorized, chunked computations. Incorporate a two-stage approach: fast greedy selection with approximate estimates followed by a brief, exact local refinement on a shortlist to improve quality without incurring excessive overhead.
comprehensive_reflection:
• Keywords: candidate pruning, reusing samples, vectorized updates, approximate hypervolume, local refinement.  
• Advice: Prune candidates early, reuse sampled data in vectorized operations, and apply approximate evaluations to quickly shortlist promising options.  
• Avoid: Repeatedly calling expensive exact hypervolume computations on the full candidate set.  
• Explanation: This approach minimizes computational overhead by focusing detailed evaluations only on a refined shortlist, balancing efficiency and solution quality.
